description: Common wild animals found across most settings — predators, prey, and woodland creatures.
creatures: Wolf
A grey hunter that moves in silent packs, circling prey with eerie coordination.
Bear
A hulking beast that rears on hind legs and swats with claws that shatter bone.
Eagle
A keen-eyed raptor that dives from impossible heights to snatch prey in its talons.
Stag
A proud deer with a crown of branching antlers, swift and watchful at the forest's edge.
Boar
A bristle-backed pig that charges with razor tusks and refuses to back down.
Lion
A tawny predator with a thundering roar that carries across the savanna.
Tiger
A striped ambush hunter that melts into tall grass and strikes in a single explosive leap.
Elephant
A towering grey giant that never forgets a face and mourns its dead.
Owl
A wide-eyed hunter that glides on silent wings through moonlit darkness.
Raven
A glossy black bird that watches with unsettling intelligence and remembers every slight.
Hawk
A swift raptor that folds its wings and plummets from the sky like a feathered arrow.
Bat
A leathery-winged night flier that swoops through darkness guided by echoing clicks.
Spider
An eight-legged weaver that spins silk traps and injects dissolving venom into struggling prey.
Scorpion
A desert arthropod with pincers and a venomous stinger curled over its segmented back.
Snake
A coiling, fanged reptile that strikes without warning from tall grass or rocky crevices.
Crocodile
An armored reptile that drifts like a log, then explodes from the water with bone-crushing jaws.
Shark
A sleek ocean predator that smells a drop of blood from miles away and never stops swimming.
Octopus
A soft-bodied genius with eight grasping arms that changes color and squeezes through impossibly tight gaps.
Giant Rat
A dog-sized rodent with yellowed fangs that carries plague and fights with cornered-animal fury.
Dire Wolf
A wolf the size of a horse, its deep howl enough to rattle bones.
Giant Spider
A massive arachnid that spins webs large enough to snare a grown human.
Giant Eagle
A colossal raptor intelligent enough to serve as a mount or an ally in battle.
Fox
A russet-furred trickster that slips through hedgerows and outwits traps with ease.
Rabbit
A long-eared herbivore that digs hidden warrens and escapes predators in explosive, zigzagging bursts.
Mouse
A tiny, twitching rodent that squeezes through the smallest cracks and vanishes in a heartbeat.
Deer
A wide-eyed forest dweller that freezes at the snap of a twig, then bounds away in silence.
Frog
A small amphibian with powerful legs for leaping and a sticky tongue for snatching insects mid-flight.
Turtle
A patient, armored reptile that outlives kingdoms and retreats into its shell at the first sign of danger.
Crow
A glossy black bird that solves puzzles, holds grudges, and gathers in ominous, chattering murders.
Monkey
An agile primate with clever hands that swings through canopies and steals anything shiny.
Otter
A playful, sleek swimmer with dense fur and nimble paws that crack shellfish on river stones.
Badger
A stocky burrower with powerful claws and a ferocious temper far larger than its body.
Panther
A sleek shadow-cat with golden eyes that stalks prey in near-total darkness.
Hedgehog
A small spiny mammal that curls into a bristling ball when anything draws too close.
Squirrel
A bushy-tailed acrobat that leaps between branches and stashes food it never quite remembers.
Raccoon
A masked nocturnal scavenger with dexterous paws that can open latches and raid anything.
Penguin
A flightless seabird in tuxedo plumage that waddles on land and rockets through icy water.
Beaver
A flat-tailed engineer that fells trees with its teeth and reshapes entire rivers with its dams.
Porcupine
A quill-covered rodent that rattles its spines in warning before backing into attackers.
Chipmunk
A tiny striped bolt of energy with cheeks stuffed full of stolen seeds.
Koala
A round, drowsy tree-hugger that sleeps twenty hours a day and grumbles when disturbed.
Sparrow
A small, hardy songbird found near every settlement, chirping from rooftops at dawn.
Crane
A tall, elegant wading bird that dances with outstretched wings during courtship.
Dove
A gentle bird with soft cooing calls and devoted pair bonds that last a lifetime.
Flamingo
A tall wading bird with vivid pink plumage and a distinctive one-legged stance.
Toad
A warty, squat amphibian with toxic skin glands that make most predators spit it out.
Heron
A tall wading bird with a spear-like bill that stands perfectly still, then strikes like lightning.
